sentences.text: Um, so the content being relevant to the property, uh, first and foremost, they want the all content for for Google profiles to be relevant to the business.
So, um, all of the posts that we do, you will see there's a difference in the post.

Um, then they also look at uniqueness of the content.
So, yeah, are you posting the same thing over and over?
Are you cross posting from social, or is it truly unique to the Google profile?
And then frequency.
So, we, uh, I'll skip forward here a little bit, um, the tool itself, so everything that we have built, um, updates at least once a day.
We have some property management softwares that we're updating the pricing availability more than once a day.
Do you use the same property management software across all properties?
Yeah, we have our own instance of RealPage now under Crescent, and we require all of our properties to use that.

OK.

Uh, it's also built to automate this entire process, so, um, you don't have to spend time posting or updating pricing, uh, we really handle all of it for you.
Now that's in the product section, right?

that has changed as well.

It is, it is a random order and it's all based on when you last updated the pricing.
So that is one limitation with Google is it is sorted by bedroom count, but you'll see when this restarts.
When it says like one bedroom, that is in order of when you updated the pricing last.
So whatever the most recent pricing update is what's gonna show first, yeah.
So if you make increases, those are gonna show up first.
Yep, all right.

Is that a deterrent for you?
